perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW and all of their subsidiaries and related companies.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW, however does not necessarily apply to all of their subsidiaries and related companies and can only be applied to particular entities as determined by ABW.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the amendment applies to all subsidiaries, the modified version limits it only to particular entities decided by ABW, potentially creating a dispute about if the subsidiaries are included in the context of the agreement which can create uncertainty about the enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This amendment to Section 2 (titled "Term"), Part B of the Co-Branding Agreement is made effective December 9, 1996 by and between PC Quote, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "ABW"), who are also the parties contracted in the aforementioned Co-Branding Agreement. This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW, however does not necessarily apply to all of their subsidiaries and related companies and can only be applied to particular entities as determined by ABW.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the amendment applies to all subsidiaries, whereas the changed text limits it only to particular entities decided by ABW. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means the changed text introduces a direct contradiction with the original intent of the clause that it's replacing. The original clause suggested all subsidiaries and related companies would be part of the agreement, and the modified text makes it conditional and subject to ABW's discretion, creating uncertainty and a potential dispute about which subsidiaries are actually included in the context of the agreement. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as per the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.
